channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: June Lockhart, beloved mother figure from 'Lassie' and 'Lost In Space,' dies at 100
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — June Lockhart, who became a mother figure for a generation of television viewers whether at home in “Lassie” or up in the stratosphere in “Lost In Space,” has died. She was 100.
item.dc:creator: Bob Thomas, ABC 4

item.title: Woman convicted of stealing $11M meant to feed St. Louis-area children
item.description: A former St. Louis County nonprofit leader was convicted of stealing $11 million from a program intended to feed low-income children in the St. Louis region.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI indicts dozens of people in Philadelphia on drug charges
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — More than two dozen people have been indicted on drug-related charges as part of a yearslong investigation into a gang in Philadelphia, the Federal Bureau of Investigation announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: How the growing penny shortage may impact you
item.description: Several retailers have started to roll out policies as they run out of pennies. Here's what to expect if you're paying with cash.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dramatic video shows NYPD officers rescuing a missing dog named Moose from beneath a Manhattan subway train
item.description: NYPD officers rescued a small dog named Moose from beneath a Manhattan subway train at 14th Street station. Dramatic bodycam footage shows the heroic rescue mission.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump speaks at tele-rally to boost GOP's chances in governor's race in New Jersey
item.description: President Donald Trump is urging his supporters in New Jersey to vote for Republican gubernatorial candidate Jack Ciattarelli.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 3 convicted for illegal BASE jumping in Yosemite National Park
item.description: “We do not tolerate illegal activity in Yosemite National Park,” Yosemite National Park Superintendent Raymond McPadden said.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: Cholesterol medication recalled over 'failed dissolution specifications': What to know
item.description: Over 141,000 bottles of cholesterol medication have been recalled due to “failed dissolution specifications,” suggesting that the drugs did not pass a solubility test, or failed to dissolve at a standard rate.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How Hispanics' views of Trump have changed since January, according to a new AP-NORC poll
item.description: President Donald Trump's favorability has fallen among Hispanic adults since the beginning of the year, a new AP-NORC poll shows, a potential warning sign from a key constituency that helped secure his victory in the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Poll: California's Prop 50 likely to pass, Republican surges in governor's race
item.description: With less than two weeks before election day, California voters appear poised to pass the state’s redistricting measure, Proposition 50, to counter Republican redistricting efforts in Texas to gain more seats in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WGN 9

item.title: Dashcam video shows deadly freeway crash involving suspected illegal immigrant trucker
item.description: Federal officials warned California about truck license compliance issues before deadly freeway crash involving suspected illegal immigrant driver killed three.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palisades Fire suspect has courtroom outburst while pleading not guilty at arraignment
item.description: Jonathan Rinderknecht, 29, who is facing charges for allegedly starting what became the devastating Palisades Fire, reportedly had a courtroom outburst.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alaska Airlines operations resume after ground stop over IT outage
item.description: Alaska Airlines said its operations have resumed Friday after it issued a temporary ground stoppage Thursday evening amid an IT outage.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, KRON4

item.title: Louisiana jury awards more than $40 million to family of man who died in privately-run jail
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A federal jury found a private company running a Louisiana jail liable for the 2015 death of a man who died of head injuries he received while in custody, and awarded the family more than $40 million in damages. Attorneys representing Erie Moore Sr.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Virginia Democrats will try to reshape US House districts in counter to Trump's redistricting push
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — Virginia Democrats are taking steps to redraw their state’s U.S. House districts, hoping to boost their party’s chances in next year’s midterm elections and counter President Donald Trump’s push for more partisan districts in Republican-run states.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 egg brands recalled for possible salmonella contamination
item.description: A recall issued last week has now expanded to include two brands of eggs that may be contaminated with salmonella.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Eric Adams endorses once bitter rival Andrew Cuomo in New York City's mayoral race
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams endorsed Andrew Cuomo in the city’s mayoral election Thursday, throwing his weight behind a bitter rival as he cast the Democratic nominee, Zohran Mamdani, as an avatar for radicals and gentrifiers.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump backs off planned surge of federal agents into San Francisco after talking to the mayor
item.description: ALAMEDA, Calif. (AP) — President Donald Trump said Thursday that he’s backing off a planned surge of federal agents into San Francisco to quell crime after speaking to the mayor and several prominent business leaders who said they're working hard to clean up the city.
item.dc:creator: TERRY CHEA and CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Illinois gubernatorial candidate's son, daughter-in-law and grandchildren killed in helicopter crash
item.description: Darren Bailey, a former state senator, officially kicked off his campaign last month to regain the Republican nomination for governor after losing to Democratic Gov. JB Pritzker in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, Fox 5

item.title: 53 percent disapprove of White House East Wing demolition: Poll
item.description: A majority of Americans said in a new poll they disapprove of President Trump’s plans to demolish part of the East Wing in the White House to make room for his $250 million ballroom.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Where the jewels stolen from the Louvre Museum might end up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just days after a stunning heist at the Louvre Museum in Paris, speculation is growing around where the lavish, stolen jewels that once adorned France's royals might end up.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and R.J. RICO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Entire White House East Wing now demolished as Trump moves forward with ballroom construction
item.description: The entire White House East Wing has been demolished as President Donald Trump moves forward with a ballroom construction, Associated Press photos on Thursday showed.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Latest: US strikes 2 more alleged drug-carrying boats, this time in the Pacific Ocean
item.description: The U.S. military on Wednesday launched its ninth strike against an alleged drug-carrying vessel, killing three people in the eastern Pacific Ocean, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said, expanding the Trump administration’s campaign against drug trafficking in South America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: How inflatable costumes ballooned at anti-Trump rallies due to a protester's frog outfit
item.description: Puffed-out costumes — hippos in tutus, Mr. Potato Heads, dinosaurs — have quickly become a feature of protests against President Donald Trump’s administration, including the massive "No Kings" marches across the U.S. last weekend.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 5 times the White House underwent major renovations
item.description: President Trump's construction of a grand ballroom attached to the White House — starting with the demolition of part of the East Wing this week — will not be the first time a president has left a lasting mark on the people's house.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Vendors on NYC's Canal Street say they were harassed and asked to show papers in immigration sweep
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A day after a mass of federal agents questioned street vendors and sparked protests on Manhattan's Canal Street, sellers were scarce on the busy strip. Some who did venture out Wednesday, though, were disheartened or riled up by a sweep in which they said people, including U.S.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Korea has stolen billions in cryptocurrency and tech firm salaries, report says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — North Korean hackers have pilfered billions of dollars by breaking into cryptocurrency exchanges and creating fake identities to get remote tech jobs at foreign companies, according to an international report on North Korea's cyber capabilities.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Spirit Christmas is expanding: 30 locations announced across 13 states
item.description: (NEXSTAR/WHTM) – It may seem like Spirit Halloween has only just taken over your nearest shuttered Rite Aid and Party City locations, but some may soon become Spirit Christmas locations within weeks.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: An elephant family smashed pumpkins at the Oregon Zoo. But this baby just wanted to play ball
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — A baby elephant at the Oregon Zoo had more tricks than treats to show when handlers gave it a small pumpkin to play with during an annual fall event where giant elephants smash half-ton pumpkins.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Appeals court overturns conviction of Colombian ex-President Uribe for bribery and witness tampering
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — An appeals court on Tuesday overturned the conviction of former Colombian President Álvaro Uribe for bribery and witness tampering for which he had been sentenced to 12 years of house arrest. Uribe, 73, has denied any wrongdoing.
item.dc:creator: ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump nominates new Army vice chief with current general just two years into the role
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has nominated Lt. Gen. Christopher LaNeve to serve as the Army’s second-highest-ranking officer, according to congressional records. Gen. James Mingus is currently vice chief of staff and has not publicly said he plans to step aside.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: American chess grandmaster Daniel Naroditsky dies at 29
item.description: Daniel Naroditsky, a chess grandmaster who started as a child prodigy and quickly became one of the most influential American voices in the sport, died Monday. He was 29.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
